title: 99% of exports to become duty-free with India-U.K. trade treaty: Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said the treaty stands as a testament to the Centre's pro-people diplomacy

title: Mudragada buried under mango tree at Kirlampudi home; family declines State honours
content: The Kapu leader was buried in his regular attire, with his belongings, against community custom; the family declined State honours, citing ‘harassment’ under the Naidu government, and his estranged daughter was turned away in the morning

title: Kharge calls Madhya Pradesh ‘epicentre of corruption’, cites alleged ₹1,200-crore rice scamt
content: Congress alleges nearly five lakh metric tonnes of rice had been siphoned off in a “game of profit” and claims diversion took place with the connivance of government machinery.

title: What is the row over Karur ‘temple land’? | Explained
content: When CM Vijay visited Karur on July 10, activist T.R. Ramesh accused the TVK government and the HR & CE of handing over the lands in question —valued at around ₹25,000 crore— to “encroachers"

title: Lakshadweep MP meets Kerala CM, seeks the State’s support for healthcare and connectivity improvements
content: M. Hamdullah Sayeed highlights the financial struggles islanders face when seeking treatment in Kerala. He calls for a dedicated help desk at local hospitals, improved vessel services from Beypore, and measures to combat rising drug trafficking.

title: India-U.K. DCC not applicable to Indians working in the U.K. before July 15
content: DCC came into effect on July 15, 2026 alongside the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA)

title: India–UK CETA comes into force; Telangana well poised to reap benefits
content: Pharmaceuticals and Life Sciences, electronics and precision engineering, engineering goods and industrial machinery  and chemicals and specialty chemicals are set to benefit most from the landmark agreement that came into force on July 15

title: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal? | Explained
content: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides. Here’s the complete picture

title: Cabinet approves ₹1.27 lakh crore for Semicon Mission 2.0
content: The government expects the new scheme to attract investments of around ₹4 lakh crore and lead to semiconductor production worth ₹2 lakh crore during the scheme period

title: Construction worker dies of asphyxiation in Kozhikode
content: Another man, who attempted to rescue the victim and fell unconcious inside the tank, was pulled out alive by the rescue squad members and admitted to a nearby hospital.

title: As Sonam Wangchuk hangs on to hunger strike, weight loss approaches 9 kg
content: On the eighteenth day, activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his hunger strike demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, even as his vital signs showed a worsening condition.

title: Assam allows restaurants to remain open till 3:30 a.m. for football World Cup matches
content: "I have authorised the district administration in Guwahati to extend restaurant operating hours till 3:30 a.m. on July 15, 16, 19 and 20 to facilitate fans to watch the matches," Assam CM

title: Form panel to find permanent solution for Inam land disputes, CPI(M) urges Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: P. Shanmugam urged the Tamil Nadu government to file a review petition before the Supreme Court and expedite the implementation of the provisions of the Forest Rights Act.

title: India joins the FIFA momentum
content: The FIFA World Cup excitement continues to build in India as fans gear up for four high-stakes knockout clashes.

title: Supreme Court raises students’ ‘frustration’ over CBSE OSM glitches with Govt., seeks status report
content: The petition seeks individual responsibility to be fixed for a failure which affected a large number of students in India and abroad; it lists several irregularities 'systematic negligence/failure resulting in a breach of duty of care’

title: CPI has no plan to join TVK-led Tamil Nadu government: Veerapandian
content: Asked about reports that the Left partywas planning to join the government, Mr. Veerapandian told The Hindu that he had only said the party would join a government when the time came for the Left to assume power in Indian politics.

title: CPI no longer in DMK-led alliance in Tamil Nadu, says Veerapandian
content: “The current situation is not conducive for an alliance. In the coming days, when there are local body and other elections, the CPI will decide its stand. The CPI takes its own political decisions,” he said

title: Two killed, 50 rescued in fire in building in Noida village, landlord arrested
content: The fire appears to have been sparked by an electric two-wheeler being charged inside the building; it spread to nearby petrol powered two-wheelers parked in the premises

title: India-U.K. trade deal will boost India’s farmers, entrepreneurs and MSMEs, PM Modi says
content: The India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), which came into effect on July 15, has been hailed by government officials in India and the U.K., and has been welcomed by Indian industry

title: An anthem for a vanishing craft: Thazhappattu makes it to school curriculum
content: Screwpine art, which involves transforming simple leaves into green, sustainable thazhappas (mats) — a craft that was once a thriving cottage industry in Thazhava but has slowly declined due to modern challenges – receives a significant tribute in the form of Thazhappattu, a song written by Ani Mank.

title: ‘Government cruel for ignoring Wangchuk's fast,’ says Dipke, as activist completes day 18 of hunger strike
content: According to the medical bulletin issued by the CJP, Sonam Wangchuk remains "very weak" and is under 24-hour medical vigilance

title: Madan Mitra leaves Mamata Banerjee, joins rebel Trinamool camp
content: Development comes a day after the Enforcement Directorate issued summons to him and his family members in the municipal recruitment scam; Mr. Mitra announced his resignation from all posts in the Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool

title: Several Jan Suraaj leaders join BJP ahead of Bihar’s Bankipur byelection
content: Those inducted into the BJP include renowned mathematician K.C. Sinha and Ritesh Ranjan alias Bittu Singh, who had contested as Jan Suraaj candidates

title: Nagercoil custodial death: PMK demands CBI probe
content: In the two months since the change of government in Tamil Nadu, four people have died under mysterious circumstances while in police custody, Anbumani Ramdoss charged

title: Chief warder, two warders arrested for the death of a remand prisoner in Nagercoil jail
content: Sabari Varman, 34, a differently abled resident of Eeththankaadu near Suchindram, was arrested on July 9 for allegedly possessing the banned gutka and remanded in custody.  He died in the prison  there under suspicious circumstances on July 12

title: Women comprise 60% of students of 2026-28 MBA programme at VGSoM, IIT Kharagpur
content: VGSoM was set up in 1993 through the philanthropic vision of IIT Kharagpur’s distinguished alumnus Vinod Gupta. VGSoM was the first management school in the IIT system

title: Janakpuri school rape case: Delhi High Court cancels bail to female teacher
content: On June 29, the High Court had also set aside the bail granted to the main accused, the caretaker of the school, and asked him to surrender on July 1

title: Padma Shri-winning Kerala puppeteer Moozhikkal Pankajakshi passes away
content: The veteran folk artist who revived and popularised Nokkuvidya Pavakali  was undergoing treatment for age-related ailments

title: Apartment owners in Bengaluru get briefing on new law for property owners from Karnataka CM
content: He informed that the proposed Karnataka Apartment (Ownership and Management) Act, 2026 has been drafted to protect the interests of homebuyers, and ensure complete ownership rights

title: Air India crash: Draft final report will be ready by October, AAIB tells Supreme Court
content: The affidavit stated that there is “an absolute statutory prohibition” on the public disclosure of cockpit voice recordings and airborne image recordings

title: RTI Chief Commissioner denied entry into Guntur police station during surprise inspection
content: Following the refusal, the CIC returned without inspecting the police station; he later continued his scheduled inspection by visiting the nearby MPDO offices

title: Truth will prevail, wait for SIT probe, says Adityanath on Ram temple funds row
content: Yogi Adityanath hits out at the Opposition, tells people that those who did not provide electricity to Ayodhya and kept it confined to narrow lanes are now trying to insult the city

title: 2,000 TMC ft of Godavari water going to sea a colossal waste, says Chandrababu Naidu
content: Paying tribute to engineer K.L. Rao at the Pavitra Sangamam, the Chief Minister said linking the two rivers could irrigate lakhs of acres, and that the TDP had brought Godavari water into the Krishna through Pattiseema

title: Explained | What is the Ram Temple embezzlement case
content: The FIR alleges a coordinated theft operation involving these temple personnel and cash-counting staff systematically pilfered cash from donation boxes and hid temple valuables during the counting and sorting phases

title: Joseph Vijay sworn-in as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister
content: As he commenced uttering “C. Joseph Vijay ennum naan…”, the crowd erupted in celebration, with whistle sounds filling the stadium with excitement and a sense of political arrival

title: CMC directs Swiggy, Zomato to delist unlicensed food outlets, seeks details on customer review policy
content: The platforms were directed to verify and maintain the accurate geo-location of every listed food establishment and ensure that food is prepared and dispatched only from approved and registered premises

title: Transgender persons can avail of any of quota benefits available to the marginalised, government tells SC
content: The Social Justice Ministry’s response came in a contempt petition accusing the government of not implementing the directions in the 2014 judgment

title: Madras High Court questions State on providing horizontal reservation for transgender persons in government jobs
content: Justices S.M. Subramaniam and N. Senthilkumar grant time till August 4, 2026 for the Tamil Nadu government to respond

title: Watch: Analysis: Should T.V.K. do away with freebies?
content: The profligacy of the successive governments has been a key factor that contributed to the very tight fiscal situation in Tamil Nadu. In the name of welfarism, they have been overindulgent in disbursing freebies. Unfortunately, the TVK too does not seem to have learnt any lesson from the experiences of its predecessors.

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ gets a release date: CM Vijay’s swansong film to finally hit the big screen
content: The announcement finally ends the months-long drama surrounding the film’s release, which the Central Board of Film Certification halted in January

title: Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ controversy explained: From legal battles to piracy chaos
content: Here’s a comprehensive look into the legal battle behind actor-politician Vijay’s swansong film ‘Jana Nayagan’. The film, directed by H Vinoth, fell into a fresh controversy when it was leaked online recently

title: Ayodhya incident hurt faith of Ram devotees: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Adityanath on donation theft issue
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath attacked the opposition parties, particularly the Samajwadi Party, accusing them of politicising matters of faith despite their past record

title: ‘I am an eight-time MLA, won’t be threatened,’ says DKS
content: Deputy CM made this statement during his interaction with representatives of Bangalore Apartments’ Federation (BAF) at Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru

title: Telangana Govt to replace Bhu Bharati’s software and architecture; new system likely in three months
content: Bhu Bharati portal was operating on the old software and architecture of Dharani portal, with some changes

title: Bengaluru: Stakeholders’ meeting on Karnataka’s Apartment Bill to be held on July 15
content: The apartment owners have been urging the government for the enactment of the Bill to bring more clarity regarding ownership and management of apartments in the State.

title: Kerala plans to start electric boat service through Parvathy Puthanar ahead of Onam
content: The initiative, spearheaded by the Transport department, aims to revitalise the 18th-century waterway as part of the larger Kovalam–Bekal National Waterway project, providing a scenic route for commuters and tourists alike.

title: Supriya Sule calls delimitation row a ‘storm in a teacup’, denies backing proposal
content: Along with a 50% increase in seats for all States, there should be clarity about the process as well, says NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule

title: For justice around the clock | Explained
content: The Supreme Court has decided to examine a writ petition for a framework to urgently set up Benches at any time of the day; the petition argues that in a system where executive power may operate at any hour, the protection of Fundamental Rights cannot remain constrained by the temporal boundaries of court schedules

title: Kejriwal to visit CJP protest at Jantar Mantar on July 16
content: "Sonam Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for some days, and his health is deteriorating. He is an asset of the country, and I appeal to him to end his hunger strike. There are other ways also for the struggle," says Arvind Kejriwal

title: Concerned over civilian deaths, Mirwaiz urges Pakistan to prioritise dialogue, restraint in PoK
content: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq also expressed “deep sorrow and pain over reports of the loss of civilian lives and police personnel in the continuing unrest on the other side of the LoC

title: Man punches glass door at Ghaziabad police booth after waiting 40 mins, bleeds to death
content: A 22-year-old man died from bleeding outside a police booth after no response. He injured his hand punching a glass door in frustration while seeking help. Two women constables inside the booth did not open the door due to his aggressive behavior. A police patrol arrived and took the injured man to the hospital where he was declared dead.

title: 'Next comes bridges, power plants': Trump says US strikes on Iran to escalate in 3 days
content: President Trump convened a meeting to discuss expanding military operations against Iran. The United States is currently conducting strikes targeting Iranian air defense systems. Trump warned of intensified attacks on power plants and bridges if Iran does not negotiate. Washington is also monitoring a deeply buried site believed to be part of Iran's nuclear program. Iran has continued retaliatory attacks on US military bases in the region.

title: ICC introduces 'Super Series' and 'Super 7' format for 2027 ODI World Cup
content: The International Cricket Council has approved significant format changes for its major men's tournaments. The ODI World Cup will now feature a three-stage format before the knockout rounds begin. The T20 World Cup will expand to twenty teams with a new Super 10 stage. A new qualification pathway for the 2028 T20 World Cup has also been approved by the council.

title: Here's why Hyderabad's monsoon tanker demand is higher than summer's
content: During the monsoon season in Hyderabad, there is a notable spike in the need for water tankers due to below-average rainfall and the effects of El Nino. Apartment complexes have increased their dependency on tanker water by three times, revealing challenges posed by fast urbanization and outdated pipeline systems. Authorities are advocating for regular water audits and the adoption of rainwater harvesting practices to secure future water supplies.

title: Google tried to sell AI chips to Nvidia's own cloud partner, Nvidia had a quiet answer
content: Google is pitching its Tensor Processing Units to neoclouds—cloud startups that rent out AI computing power—challenging Nvidia's grip on the AI chip market, reports The Information. After learning of Google's talks with Nvidia partner Nscale, Nvidia reportedly offered financial incentives said to be aimed at cooling Nscale's interest in Google's TPUs. Nscale denies any such offer. Here's how the Google-Nvidia chip war is escalating.

title: Oil, LNG, fertilisers and maybe diamonds: What is at risk from Iran’s wider blockade threat
content: Iran has warned of wider trade disruptions beyond the Strait of Hormuz. This threat could impact crucial energy and other export flows from the region. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital chokepoint for global oil and gas shipments. Asia, which receives most of these shipments, faces significant exposure. Tensions are escalating as the US conducts strikes against Iranian capabilities.

title: From Delhi to Karnataka: EC releases revised SIR schedule - check new dates
content: The Election Commission has updated the schedule for electoral roll revision in six states. Draft publication dates vary, with final rolls due in October for most. Telangana's revision will see draft publication on August 10 and finalization on October 12. Haryana and Andhra Pradesh will publish draft rolls on July 31. This exercise aims to update voter lists by adding new and removing ineligible electors.

title: Historic! Rodri breaks 60-yo record, becomes first-ever player in FIFA WC to ...
content: Spain's Rodri made FIFA World Cup history by completing 655 passes at the 2026 tournament — the most by any player in a single edition since 1966. The Ballon d'Or winner starred in Spain's 2-0 semi-final victory over France, producing a dominant midfield display as La Roja reached their first World Cup final since 2010.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: 2020 Delhi riots case: Court allows Umar Khalid 2 family video meetings every week
content: A Delhi court has restored activist Umar Khalid's weekly video meetings with his family. Jail authorities had previously reduced these meetings from two to one weekly session. The court found no justification for the reduction in the number of meetings. Khalid remains in jail in connection with the 2020 northeast Delhi riots conspiracy case. This order follows the rejection of his recent bail pleas by a Delhi court.

title: Inside Princess Margaret’s former retreat now listed for £3.95 million
content: Princess Margaret's former country estate, The Old House, is now on the market. This historic property, once owned by Lord Snowdon, is listed for £3.95 million. The estate features six bedrooms and extensive grounds in Staplefield, West Sussex. It hosted numerous royals and celebrities during its time as a royal abode. The house holds significant cultural and historical importance within British society.

title: Working late often? Psychology says it may reveal hidden stress
content: Many office workers stay late due to perfectionism and difficulty setting boundaries. Emotional exhaustion can also drive prolonged work hours as a coping mechanism. These late nights are often a sign of underlying psychological pressures. Labeling this as dedication can mask deeper issues and reinforce unhealthy patterns. Understanding these drivers is key to fostering healthier work habits.

title: Oracle rating downgraded by S&P, now stands one notch above junk; reason is Sam Altman
content: S&P Global Ratings lowered Oracle's credit rating to BBB- from BBB.  The agency cited significant risks associated with Oracle's expanding AI infrastructure business.  OpenAI's substantial reliance on Oracle's services presents a key credit risk for the company.  Oracle faces potential financial strain from massive data center leases if OpenAI defaults.  The rating agency warned of further downgrades if leverage exceeds 4.5x.

title: Setback for Prashant Kishor! 4 Jan Suraaj leaders switch to BJP before Bankipur bypoll
content: Several Jan Suraaj leaders joined the Bharatiya Janata Party on Wednesday. This development occurs before the Bankipur Assembly bypoll scheduled for July 30. Prashant Kishor is contesting this election against the BJP candidate Neeraj Kumar Sinha. New entrants expressed their commitment to Prime Minister Modi's leadership and national development. The bypoll was necessitated after Nitin Nabin resigned from the assembly seat.

title: Why Taslima Nasreen's Kolkata return is a powder keg for Bengal politics
content: Taslima Nasreen's upcoming return to Kolkata after 20 years sparks intense political discussions. The Trinamool Congress and Bharatiya Janata Party both voiced their strong opinions regarding her visit. Exiled since 2007, Nasreen is set to participate in an anti-fundamentalism literary event this August. Her previous works and the circumstances of her exile have sent shockwaves through conversations about free speech and the intersection with religious beliefs in West Bengal.

title: 2 Varanasi highway projects worth Rs 25,44 cr approved - how they will affect commuters
content: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs has greenlighted an ambitious infrastructure project in Varanasi, featuring a remarkable Rs 10,998.32 crore investment. The initiative is set to create a 43.2-km elevated corridor, referred to as the Varuna Expressway. This project aims to significantly alleviate traffic congestion while improving connectivity by linking NH-31 to the Varanasi Ring Road, ultimately reducing travel time and stimulating regional economic growth.

title: A ticket to the past: When buses of Bombay were truly the BEST
content: The article recalls a time when bus travel in Bombay was a pleasant experience. Legendary routes like the No. 123 offered scenic journeys along Marine Drive. Double-decker buses provided joy with open windows and a refreshing breeze. These buses served as vital lifelines for night shift workers across the city. Modern transit has replaced this cherished era of public transportation.

title: Can you legally record the police in India? Here's what law says
content: Citizens can generally record police officers performing duties in public spaces. Recording is legal if it does not obstruct police work or break other laws. Police cannot arbitrarily seize phones or force deletion of lawfully recorded videos. Uploading videos may lead to consequences if they are defamatory or invade privacy. Courts emphasize transparency while balancing individual rights and effective policing.

title: Everyone wants to kill Google Chrome, and OpenAI's Atlas shows why nobody can
content: Try to remember choosing your browser. You can't, and neither can anyone else. OpenAI killed ChatGPT Atlas nine months after launch, and it is only the newest name on a list that includes Arc, Comet, Opera and Edge. Everyone attacks the browser. Nobody attacks the default. Chrome does not stop working, and that, in the end, is the whole wall.

title: Inside India's esports boom: Why leagues, careers and homegrown IP matter more than ever
content: India has around 600 million gamers and one of the world's fastest-growing gaming markets. But industry leaders believe the next challenge is no longer attracting players - it is building leagues, careers, intellectual property and businesses that can sustain long-term growth. As investment, regulation and e-cricket gather momentum, Indian esports finds itself at a defining moment in its evolution.

title: 'Not offered with genuine devotion': UP assembly speaker on Ram temple stolen donations
content: Uttar Pradesh assembly speaker Satish Mahana linked donation theft to lack of genuine devotion. He stated that those who feel their money was stolen may not have offered it sincerely. An investigation revealed nine major procedural and security lapses enabling embezzlement of temple donations. Eight individuals associated with the donation counting process have been arrested so far.

title: How Spain ended 'Dictator' Mbappe's reign and blew France apart
content: Until they met Spain, Mbappe and Co were tearing defences apart with disdain, but from Spain they finally learnt the meaning of pain. Today’s OFFSIDE explains how Spain took France’s imperial swagger and tore it Bona-part.

title: Semicon 2.0, Varanasi projects, rail upgrades & more: 7 big decisions cleared by Cabinet
content: The Union Cabinet approved seven significant decisions on Wednesday. These approvals covered infrastructure development and semiconductor manufacturing initiatives. Railway network upgradation and mobile phone manufacturing schemes were also sanctioned. A National Investment Policy for Urea was approved to boost self-reliance. These decisions aim to foster growth across key sectors of the economy.

title: Drone warfare 2.0: Unmanned vehicles outfitted with long-range missiles deliver deep strikes
content: Drones are now equipped with missiles, extending their strike capabilities significantly. Turkey's Baykar Kizilelma and Russia's Orion drone demonstrate this new offensive power. India's Ghatak stealth drone program aims for domestic deep-strike missions without pilot risk. The United States deployed unmanned surface vessels offensively against Iranian naval facilities. Ukraine showcased complex autonomous operations using sea, air, and land-based unmanned systems.

title: Toyota Innova Hycross facelift spied testing: Expected changes, specs & more
content: There are no details of the interior yet. However, Toyota is expected to revise the interior with updated upholstery and trim materials to give it a fresher appearance. Expected features include a bigger free-standing infotainment screen, a fully digital cluster, dual-zone climate control, ventilated seats, a wireless charger, a panoramic sunroof, electric seats adjustable for front passengers and much more.

title: Who is Rafael Jodar? Spanish teen joins Team Europe for Laver Cup 2026
content: Spanish youngster Rafael Jodar is quickly becoming one of the brightest rising stars in men’s tennis. The 19-year-old has enjoyed a breakthrough season in 2026, climbing the ATP rankings and earning a place alongside some of the biggest names in the sport. His rapid rise has now been rewarded with a call-up to Team Europe for the 2026 Laver Cup in London.

title: Doctors report rise in Influenza A, Covid-19 and H1N1 this monsoon, urge caution
content: Respiratory infections like Influenza A and COVID-19 are increasing during the monsoon. Doctors advise awareness and proper medical treatment for these common infections. Vulnerable groups should seek prompt medical evaluation if symptoms worsen. Unnecessary antibiotic use contributes to antimicrobial resistance, experts warn. Simple precautions and timely testing help prevent severe complications.

title: Why do eggs in supermarkets come only in white and brown shells?
content: Eggs that are available in the supermarket shelves are mostly while and brown. While the debate around the fact that which one is better continues, the question is why are they of this colour only and why commonly colourful eggs are not consumed.

title: How Mumbai's expanding Metro network is changing homebuyer preferences
content: Metro connectivity is increasingly influencing property values in India's largest cities. Proximity to metro stations now significantly impacts apartment prices more than traditional addresses. Mumbai's property market shows a clear shift towards connectivity-adjusted value for buyers. Gurgaon's transformation into a real estate hotspot exemplifies this transit-driven appreciation. Understanding future metro routes is becoming crucial for property investment decisions.

title: 'I would've done the same': Tuchel on Argentina's dark blue jersey choice for WC semis
content: England coach Thomas Tuchel understands Argentina's choice of dark blue jersey for the World Cup semifinal. He acknowledges the role of superstition and lucky charms in elite sports.  Argentina's dark blue kit holds historical significance for past victories against England.  Coach Lionel Scaloni dismissed superstition, suggesting tradition influenced the decision.

title: Delhi HC seeks govt's response on plea seeking medical intervention for Sonam Wangchuk
content: The Delhi High Court has sought responses from the Centre and Delhi government regarding a PIL. This plea requests urgent medical intervention for climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who is on an indefinite hunger strike. The activist-lawyer Rakesh Kumar Saini filed the public interest litigation seeking hospitalisation and supervised treatment. Wangchuk has been fasting since June 28, demanding the Education Minister's resignation over examination irregularities.

title: Jana Nayagan: Vijay film clears CBFC A certificate; theatrical release on July 23
content: The makers of Vijay starrer Jana Nayagan have announced its release date. The film will now be released in theaters in just a couple of days. It faced delays due to censorship and an online leak previously. Jana Nayagan received an 'A' certificate from the CBFC. The movie features Vijay as a people's leader and former police officer.

title: Why India needs amphibious combat boats against Pakistan - the unique challenges of Sir Creek
content: India and Pakistan maintain a territorial dispute over the Sir Creek estuary. Pakistan claims the entire creek, while India asserts the middle as the boundary. India is strengthening its presence with new amphibious combat boats for patrols. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh recently visited the sensitive sector. Both nations regularly arrest fishermen for alleged boundary crossings.

title: Alia Bhatt, Swara Bhasker & more rally behind Sonam Wangchuk to end hunger strike
content: Film personalities are urging activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike. Concerns are growing over his deteriorating health after weeks of fasting. Shabana Azmi and Soni Razdan have appealed for his well-being and continued leadership. Other celebrities and writers also joined calls for him to end the fast. Wangchuk is protesting alleged NEET examination irregularities and demanding minister's resignation.

title: Massive blaze engulfs Gurgaon’s Kingdom of Dreams, several fire tenders deployed
content: A significant fire erupted at the disused Kingdom of Dreams complex in Gurgaon's Sector 29. Firefighters responded swiftly to the large blaze, which produced thick smoke visible from afar. The fire department noticed the smoke because its office is located nearby. Officials are investigating the cause of the fire, which has not yet been determined. Fortunately, no injuries were reported as the facility was empty and closed.

title: EV battery behind Noida building fire? 2 dead, dozens rescued
content: A fire erupted in a Noida residential building on Wednesday. Two individuals tragically lost their lives in the Mamura village incident. Several residents are feared trapped inside the burning structure. Local residents captured videos showing thick smoke billowing from the building. People on the terrace used a ladder to escape the dangerous situation.

title: 'Bumrah cannot be the answer to all your problems': Gautam Gambhir warned
content: India began the ODI series with a six-wicket victory against England. Jasprit Bumrah and Prasidh Krishna led the bowling attack effectively. Joe Root and Liam Dawson provided a strong recovery for the hosts. Shubman Gill and Axar Patel guided India to a successful chase. The team took a 1-0 lead in the three-match series.

title: Big blow to Mamata! Another TMC leader Madan Mitra quits, joins rebel camp
content: Trinamool Congress MLA Madan Mitra has resigned from Mamata Banerjee's faction. He joined the rebel TMC faction led by Ritabrata Banerjee on Wednesday. Mitra accused Abhishek Banerjee of refusing to step aside during the party's crisis. This development follows summons issued to Mitra's family by the Enforcement Directorate. The ED is investigating a municipal recruitment scam in West Bengal.

title: Nashik highway horror: New video shows attacker hanging onto moving car as family tries to escape
content: A disturbing new video captures the moment a man clings precariously to the speeding SUV of a tourist family. According to their claims, the incident began after a woman protested against harassment. During the harrowing chase, the attackers reportedly rammed their vehicle and broke windows while the couple's young daughters watched helplessly. Law enforcement is now probing this serious case that unfolded on the highway.

title: Meet the Indian artist who cycled from India to Sweden for love in the 1970s
content: An Indian art student met a Swedish traveler in Delhi, and their connection grew quickly. He promised to follow her to Sweden after finishing his studies. Selling all possessions, he cycled thousands of kilometers across continents to reach her. His journey involved overcoming many hardships and relying on his art for survival. The couple eventually married and built a life together in Sweden.

title: 'We're coming for you': Lamine Yamal sends Spain World Cup final warning
content: Spain's teenage sensation Lamine Yamal expressed confidence after reaching the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.  He posted a message indicating their intention to win the championship.  Spain secured their place by defeating France 2-0 in the semifinal match.  The team celebrated their victory while shifting focus to the upcoming championship game.  They now await their opponent in the final match to be held in New York.

title: Woman Strangled To Death By Husband In UP, Police Case Filed
content: The accused have been identified as Vijendra and his elder brother Vinod, residents of Avrai Kala Pahi village in Ballia's Bhimpura area.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral Highlights: Iran Begins Ali Khamenei's Funeral Procession through Tehran
content: Ali Khamenei's Last Rites Highlights: Authorities have shut down streets, airspace and daily life for the mourning, which will end on Thursday as the 86-year-old Khamenei is buried at the Imam Reza shrine in Mashhad, his birthplace

title: "Intend To Kill Muslims": US Man Stabs An Indian In Mall Over His Religion
content: 48-year-old Peter Michael Larsen told investigators he deliberately targeted the victim because he was Muslim and that he "intends to kill Muslims".

title: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Highlights: Vietnam Orders Probe After Boat Tragedy Kills 15 Indian Tourists
content: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Highlights: The speedboat was ferrying 32 Indian tourists, three crew members and one Vietnamese tour guide from May Rut Ngoai Islet to An Thoi International Port

title: 'Not Essential': Kenyan Court Denies Rastafarians' Right to Smoke Weed
content: Rastafarians have been pushing Kenyan courts since 2021 to allow them to use the herb under the constitutional right to freedom of religion.

title: Government File Delayed? Delhi's New Bill Could Fine The Officer Responsible
content: The proposed law will give citizens a legal right to receive notified government services within fixed timelines and track their applications online.

title: How Delhi Police Proved Tahir Hussain Was Behind Intel Officer's Killing
